propositions: Repoint the global signing.sign_manifest seam so all producers mint rebar.opcert.v1 Ed25519 DSSE op-certs with the environment key instead of legacy per-clone HMAC secrets, yielding one signature per verdict.
On first signing use, auto-generate a passphrase-free Ed25519 keypair at .opcert-key/.opcert-key.pub race-safely via a 0700 staging dir and os.link exclusive-create commit point (never os.replace).
Add an expand-phase read-both dispatch: a shape-aware verify wrapper routes envelope-bearing records to the op-cert verifier and legacy signature-bearing records to the unchanged HMAC verify path.
Classify a record whose principal differs from the environment env_id as foreign_key without invoking the scheme, matching existing HMAC foreign_key semantics.
Update the sign-result and verify-result schema contracts (sign_result.schema.json, SignResultOut, SignResult TypedDict, verify verdict enum) to admit both HMAC and envelope shapes.

When ssh-keygen is unavailable or the key is unreadable and unregenerable, signing records an in-band {signed:false} outcome without blocking local operations, and the gate reports a missing-attestation block naming OpenSSH >= 8.9.
